Sea slugs: photo index

molluscs text index | photo index Phylum Mollusca

Class Gastropod > sea slugs Photo index of sea slugs on Singapore shores
slugs with external gills: small <5cm

Tiny black
gymnodoris Gymnodoris sp.

Yellow-spotted
gymnodoris Gymnodoris sp.


Reliable
nudibranch Goniobranchus fidelis.

Orange-spotted
gymnodoris Gymnodoris rubropapulosa

1cm or less. Body black with yellow spots. Seaweeds and seagrasses. Sometimes seen on some of our Northern shores. 2-5cm. Body white, yellow, orange with tiny yellow spots. Seagrass meadows and seaweeds. Sometimes seen on our shores. 2-5cm. Body broad white with undulating red edge pattern. Rhinophores and gills tipped in black. Coral rubble and reefs. Sometimes seen on our shores.   2-5cm. Body white, large orange spots, rhinophores orange and gills edged in orange. Coral rubble. Sometimes seen on our Southern shores.


Lined
chromodoris nudibranch Chromodoris lineolata


Pink-gilled hypselodoris
nudibranch Hypselodoris sp.


Kanga hypselodoris
nudibranch Hypselodoris kanga


Maritima hypselodoris
nudibranch Hypselodoris maritima

2-3cm. Body long with broad tail, black with fine white lines. Black line along the inner edge of the orange border on the foot. Coral rubble. Seasonally common on our Southern shores. 2-3cm. Body long, narrow with a long tail. Pale with small blue and orange spots, gills pink-edged without spots. Coral rubble. Sometimes seen on our Northern shores. 2-3cm. Body long, narrow with a long tail. Pale with small blue and orange spots, blue bars, gill triangular cross-section with orange spots. Coral rubble. Sometimes seen on our Northern shores. 2-3cm. Body long, narrow with a long tail. Pale with black blotches, yellow margin, pink-edged gills. Coral rubble. Sometimes seen on some of our shores.


Maroon cow
nudibranch Goniobranchus tumulifera


Black cow
nudibranch Chromodoris orientalis

2-3cm. Body broad white with maroon spots. Edge of body yellow with maroon spots, gills and rhinophores white. Coral rubble. Sometimes seen on our Northern shores. 2-3cm. Body broad white with black spots. Edge of body orange no black spots, gills and rhinophores orange. Silty shores. Sometimes seen on our Northern shores.      

how to tell apart

  • flatworms and slugs
  • nudibranchs, sea hares and sap-sucking slugs
  • hairy slugs and snails

molluscs on this site

body features
